title: Support `containers-storage:` with `sha256:` prefix
body:
The code was previously trying to interpret this as a named reference and landing up turning sha256:xyz into docker.io/library/sha256:xyz and failing to find it.

That's very clearly not what the user intended in this case, so let's just use the digest value directly.

Fixes #2750

comments.body: I have no idea what I'm doing here, but this seems to fix it :upside_down_face:

Hi @allisonkarlitskaya , I'm not opposed to it, but I'll defer to @mtrmac . If we do decide to get this in, this should first go in as a PR to https://github.com/containers/container-libs and have that vendored here (updating go.mod and running `make vendor`)

> This fairly strongly depends on what we do for the sha512 support. (Cc: @lsm5 ).

@mtrmac  by `we`, do you ultimately mean image-spec here? Any changes of that sort would first have to be approved by image-spec, isn't that the case?

No, `opencontainers/image-spec` defines the external (on-registry / in-files) representation of images. Image IDs are an implementation / UX choice of c/storage+Podman and friends; `opencontainers/image-spec` does not have image IDs at all.

> And then there’s the zstd:chunked problem space (`insecure_allow_unpredictable_image_contents`) where we _already_ don’t want to say that image ID equal config digests.

(We can have this conversation elsewhere I guess) What's being done for image IDs in zstd:chunked in that case? And can (should) we use the same approach across the board?

> What's being done for image IDs in zstd:chunked in that case?

Normally we enforce, after pulling zstd:chunked layers, that they match the diffID in config; and we use image ID = config digest. That comes at a fairly high performance cost.

Originally the design was not to do that; but then we must not deduplicate chunked-pulled and tar-pulled images (because we don’t know for sure the contents/views are the same), so they must have different image IDs, i.e. zstd:chunked-pulled images must have IDs that are deterministic (related to how the image was pulled) but _not_ a config digest (but happen to be a sha256 of some different value, that’s not documented).

> And can (should) we use the same approach across the board?

We had to abandon the original design and add the costly checks because the thought was that the Podman userbase is not ready for image IDs that are not equal to config digests. So directly, and universally, using the chunked design is not an immediate option.

[FWIW containerd now uses image ID = _manifest_ digest, not _config_ digest; so there _is_ a precedent for changing the value, of sorts.]

I was thinking that with the move to sha512, we _have_ the opportunity to change the image ID format — something will _certainly_ need to change because we can’t fit a sha512 digest into a sha256-sized-and-formatted identifier, and using a sha256 digest (or truncating a sha512 one) would not give us a sha512 level of collision resistance. But for old sha256 images (pulled using a sha256 configuration, whatever that means??) we will probably need to keep using the current deterministic values for a _long_ time.

> the thought was that the Podman userbase is not ready for image IDs that are not equal to config digests.

Is that thought still true?

> [FWIW containerd now uses image ID = _manifest_ digest, not _config_ digest; so there _is_ a precedent for changing the value, of sorts.]

If containerd uses it, then maybe podman is ok as well? Or are there other concerns?

> I was thinking that with the move to sha512, we _have_ the opportunity to change the image ID format — something will _certainly_ need to change because we can’t fit a sha512 digest into a sha256-sized-and-formatted identifier, and using a sha256 digest (or truncating a sha512 one) would not give us a sha512 level of collision resistance. But for old sha256 images (pulled using a sha256 configuration, whatever that means??) we will probably need to keep using the current deterministic values for a _long_ time.

do you see any of this as a blocker to the in-progress stuff for sha512, or are we ok to make changes after the initial sha512 support is introduced? FWIW https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2747

> > the thought was that the Podman userbase is not ready for image IDs that are not equal to config digests.
> 
> Is that thought still true?

I mean, we rolled that back so we have no data proving that this is reasonable to do. And we did nothing at all to prepare users for ID changes since the zstd:chunked addition, so assuming it was true last year, it almost certainly continues to be true.

> > I was thinking that with the move to sha512, we _have_ the opportunity to change the image ID format — something will _certainly_ need to change because we can’t fit a sha512 digest into a sha256-sized-and-formatted identifier, and using a sha256 digest (or truncating a sha512 one) would not give us a sha512 level of collision resistance. But for old sha256 images (pulled using a sha256 configuration, whatever that means??) we will probably need to keep using the current deterministic values for a _long_ time.
> 
> do you see any of this as a blocker to the in-progress stuff for sha512

I’m not sure which parts count as “in-progress” right now; we need to make _some_ choices for c/storage IDs to implement sha512 there.

> or are we ok to make changes after the initial sha512 support is introduced?

Well if we chose and documented that image IDs = sha512 digests of $something, and users started relying on that, we would not be at liberty to break that again. If we chose and documented that image IDs have unspecified contents, we can change, and add more commitments, later.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
As discussed above, I don’t know what design we end up with, but right now now, we certainly don’t want to commit to _this_ promise.
